sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value INTEGER, data_list ARRAY<INTEGER>);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, data_list) VALUES (1, 10, [2, 3, 4]), (2, NULL, [5, 6, 7]), (3, 255, []);
SELECT id, array_push_front(1, data_list) AS new_list FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (value << 2) AS shifted_value FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY shifted_value;
SELECT t1.id, array_push_front(1 << t1.value, t2.data_list) FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t2.value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (array_push_front(value, data_list) << 1) FROM test_table HAVING shifted_value > 5;
WITH prep_table AS (SELECT id, array_push_front(value << 1, data_list) AS modified_list FROM test_table) SELECT * FROM prep_table WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL);
CREATE TABLE another_table (another_id INTEGER, another_value STRING);
SELECT t1.another_id, array_push_front(another_value << 1, [3]) FROM another_table t1 WHERE another_value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY another_id;
